steps.label: Personal
steps.title: Personal information
steps.subtitle: Used to create your patient record and verify your identity with our licensed provider team.

steps.label: Metrics
steps.title: Body metrics & goals
steps.subtitle: Your provider uses this to evaluate clinical appropriateness and set your initial dosing baseline.
steps.bmiNote: A BMI ≥ 27 with at least one weight-related comorbidity (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, etc.), or a BMI ≥ 30, is the standard eligibility threshold for tirzepatide therapy.

steps.label: History
steps.title: Medical history
steps.subtitle: Select all conditions that currently apply or that you have a history of. Your provider reviews this before approving your order.

contraindications.warning: Tirzepatide carries an FDA black-box warning for thyroid C-cell tumors (medullary thyroid carcinoma / MEN2 syndrome). A personal or family history of MTC or MEN2 is a contraindication. Your provider will review your intake and may not be able to approve this order.
A history of acute or chronic pancreatitis requires close provider review. Tirzepatide may be contraindicated depending on the severity and recency of your history.
Moderate-to-severe chronic kidney disease requires provider review before tirzepatide can be prescribed. Dosing adjustments may be necessary.

steps.label: Medications
steps.title: Current medications & allergies
steps.subtitle: Include all prescription medications, OTC drugs, and supplements. This helps your provider identify interactions before approving your order.
commonInteractions: Insulin or insulin secretagogues — combined use increases hypoglycemia risk
Warfarin / blood thinners — INR should be monitored after starting tirzepatide
Oral contraceptives — tirzepatide may delay oral absorption; backup contraception recommended for 4 weeks after each dose escalation
Thyroid medications (levothyroxine) — changes in weight can affect thyroid dosing

steps.label: GLP-1
steps.title: GLP-1 / GIP agonist history
steps.subtitle: If you've used tirzepatide or any GLP-1 medication before, your provider needs this to calibrate your starting dose and avoid re-titration side effects.
steps.returningPatientNote: Returning tirzepatide patients resuming at a dose above 2.5 mg/week may need recent bloodwork (HbA1c, CMP, lipid panel). Your provider will request this during review if applicable.

steps.label: Lifestyle
steps.title: Lifestyle
steps.subtitle: Context that helps your provider set realistic expectations, monitor progress, and personalise your care plan.

steps.label: Review
steps.title: Review & consent
steps.subtitle: Confirm your answers are accurate before submitting your intake to our provider team.

consents.label: I understand my order is subject to clinical review and may be modified or denied by a licensed provider.
I understand this service does not replace an in-person physician relationship and is not a substitute for emergency medical care.
I understand my provider may adjust my tirzepatide dose based on my medical history, even if it differs from what I configured.
I have read and agree to the cancellation and refund policy.

steps.label: Personal
steps.title: Personal information
steps.subtitle: Used to create your patient record and verify your identity with our licensed provider team.

steps.label: Metrics
steps.title: Body metrics & goals
steps.subtitle: Your provider uses this to evaluate clinical appropriateness and set your initial dosing baseline.
steps.bmiNote: A BMI ≥ 27 with at least one weight-related comorbidity, or a BMI ≥ 30, is the standard eligibility threshold for semaglutide therapy. For type 2 diabetes management, BMI criteria may differ — your provider will confirm.

steps.label: History
steps.title: Medical history
steps.subtitle: Select all conditions that currently apply or that you have a history of. Your provider reviews this before approving your order.

contraindications.warning: Semaglutide carries an FDA black-box warning for thyroid C-cell tumors (medullary thyroid carcinoma / MEN2 syndrome). A personal or family history of MTC or MEN2 is a contraindication. Your provider will review and may not be able to approve this order.
A personal history of acute or chronic pancreatitis requires close provider review. Semaglutide may be contraindicated depending on the nature of your history.
Moderate-to-severe chronic kidney disease should be disclosed. Semaglutide does not require dose adjustment for renal impairment, but your provider will review your full picture.

steps.label: Medications
steps.title: Current medications & allergies
steps.subtitle: Include all prescription medications, OTC drugs, and supplements. This helps your provider identify interactions before approving your order.
commonInteractions: Insulin or insulin secretagogues — combined use significantly increases hypoglycemia risk
Warfarin / blood thinners — INR monitoring recommended after initiating semaglutide
Oral medications requiring precise absorption timing — semaglutide delays gastric emptying
Thyroid medications (levothyroxine) — weight changes from semaglutide may affect optimal thyroid dosing

steps.label: GLP-1
steps.title: GLP-1 agonist history
steps.subtitle: If you've used semaglutide or any GLP-1 medication before, your provider needs this to calibrate your starting dose and avoid re-titration side effects.
steps.returningPatientNote: Returning semaglutide patients resuming at a dose above 0.5 mg/week may need recent labs (HbA1c, CMP). Your provider will request this during review if applicable.

steps.label: Lifestyle
steps.title: Lifestyle
steps.subtitle: Context that helps your provider set realistic expectations, monitor progress, and personalise your care plan.

steps.label: Review
steps.title: Review & consent
steps.subtitle: Confirm your answers are accurate before submitting your intake to our provider team.

consents.label: I understand my order is subject to clinical review and may be modified or denied by a licensed provider.
I understand this service does not replace an in-person physician relationship and is not a substitute for emergency medical care.
I understand my provider may adjust my semaglutide dose based on my medical history, even if it differs from what I configured.
I have read and agree to the cancellation and refund policy.
